verdict_per_deliverable.D2: load_config call remains triggerable by 6 zero-arg callers; ctx not threaded; w15 escalation rule applies. Required closure path documented below.
verdict_per_deliverable.D3: Private-method abuse fully removed. live._request → live.update_task in dashboard_maintainer.py:113-120. New named public method on LiveVikunjaClient (lines 105-115). Zero remaining (live|client)._request hits in production code per grep verification.
verdict_per_deliverable.D4: EA-5's DIVERGE from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) recommendation is well-reasoned and ACCEPTED. EA-5 caught a Guide-side reasoning flaw (chokepoint VikunjaClient class is a stub; the function _require_project_id imported at handler entry IS the chokepoint integration, not redundancy on top of it). HOWEVER, the documentation block in server.py is DUPLICATED (lines 13-57 + lines 59-103 verbatim). Required cleanup: remove the second copy.
verdict_per_deliverable.D1: Extended grep with 12 patterns is the remaining Item 2.6 deliverable. EA-5 has not run it.

guide_review_response.work_item: 2.6 inter-deliverable status review (D2/D3/D4 closure + D1 pending + v5 handoff timing)

reviewing.ea_artifact: EA-5 chat summary at 2.6 D2/D3/D4 close + v5 handoff request

guide_review_response.summary:
Independent disk verification of EA-5's D2/D3/D4 work product surfaces three findings:
(1) D3 is fully closed. The private-method abuse `live._request("POST", f"/tasks/{...}", json={...})` at dashboard_maintainer.py:113 is gone, replaced by a named public method `live.update_task(...)`. Grep across tools/ for `(live|client)\._request` returns one hit — and that hit is the comment block at dashboard_maintainer.py:113 documenting the OLD code, not active code. The new `update_task` named public method on LiveVikunjaClient (lines 105-115 of legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py) is well-documented with a Stage 2.6 D3 closure note and prefers typed wrappers like `update_task_priority` where they exist. Clean closure.
(2) D4 decision is well-reasoned and APPROVED. EA-5 chose to DIVERGE from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) recommendation and KEEP all 8 `_require_project_id(project_id)` call sites at the tops of the project-scoped MCP handlers. EA-5's rationale (4-point block in server.py docstring) catches a substantive flaw in Guide-#6's reasoning: the chokepoint VikunjaClient class in tools/_vikunja_client.py is currently a STUB (no HTTP delegation; docstring at lines 50-53 explicitly says "HTTP delegation is intentionally left as a stub at this stage; Stage 2.6 audits and migrates direct requests/httpx call sites to use this wrapper"). Server.py's HTTP path uses local `_api_*` helpers + httpx directly and never traverses VikunjaClient class methods. Therefore the function `_require_project_id` (which server.py imports from the chokepoint module and invokes at handler entry) IS the chokepoint integration — removing the handler-level calls would NOT consolidate, it would UNHOOK the server from the chokepoint at the MCP trust boundary. EA-5's framing is correct; Guide-#6's recommendation was based on a misread of the current chokepoint state. This is exactly the LA's "treat as hypothesis; surface evidence; align or diverge with documented rationale" mandate working as intended. Mature engineering.
HOWEVER, D4's documentation has a substantive defect: the entire 45-line decision block at server.py lines 13-57 is DUPLICATED VERBATIM at lines 59-103. This is an authoring artifact (likely an Edit/append operation that ran twice), analogous to g5-7's duplicate test definitions in test_vikunja_client_scope.py. Cleanup REQUIRED: remove lines 58-103 (the closing `---` line at 57 + the entire second copy at 59-103). The result is a single 44-line decision block from line 13 to line 56, then the original module imports at line 105+.
(3) D2 disposition is INCOMPLETE per ack g5-4. EA-5 made the load_config call CONDITIONAL in legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py.__init__ (line 39-41: `if base_url is None: cfg = config_loader.load_config(); base_url = cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]`) but did NOT update the 6 zero-arg callers (credential_rotation_reminder.py, welcome_back_digest.py, daily_digest.py, weekly_summary.py, dashboard_maintainer.py:93, gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py) to pass base_url explicitly. Therefore the 6 zero-arg callers STILL trigger the conditional fallback, which STILL invokes load_config() with zero args, which STILL walks cwd and auto-resolves the project overlay — exactly the post-Stage-4-cutover hazard ack g5-4 explicitly flagged. EA-5's framing in the legacy file's docstring ("g5-4 thin-shim closure of the eager config_loader.load_config() call that previously executed unconditionally") misframes the hazard: the hazard isn't EAGERNESS, it's CTX-AWARENESS. Making the call conditional doesn't help if callers still invoke it with zero args.
Per ack g5-4 explicit text: "If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide." EA-5 left the load_config call in place (it moved from line 28 to line 40 but is still there). EA-5 did NOT thread ctx (they added a base_url parameter, not a ctx parameter; the 6 callers don't use the new parameter). Per the explicit escalation rule, w15 hazard triggered. THIS REVIEW IS THAT ESCALATION.
Required D2 closure: update the 6 zero-arg callers to pass `base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]` when constructing LiveVikunjaClient. Each caller already has `cfg` resolved via `config_loader.load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/...)` (i.e., ctx-aware), so the change is a 1-line update per caller (~6 lines total). After the update, no production caller will invoke the conditional fallback, and the load_config call is effectively closed (still present in the constructor as a safety net for ad-hoc usage, but not reachable from the production fleet path).
On the v5 mid-stage handoff timing: EA-5's recommendation to emit v5 at the D2/D3/D4 → D1 boundary is sound on context-budget grounds (per i1-4 + dn15: "even slightly tight" budget at clean breakpoint = handoff). EA-5's stop-and-ask discipline is correct (no autonomous v5 emission; defer to LA + Guide). APPROVING v5 handoff CONDITIONAL on: (a) EA-5 cleans up the duplicate D4 comment block in server.py NOW (trivial; ~30 seconds of Edit-tool-deletion of lines 58-103). This is too cheap to justify deferring to EA-6, and it removes a documentation defect from the artifact set EA-6 inherits. (b) EA-5's v5 handoff XML EXPLICITLY encodes the D2 incompleteness + closure path, so EA-6 picks up D2 closure as a first action before D1. The D2 closure work itself (6-caller update; ~30-45 min) is appropriate to DEFER to EA-6 given EA-5's honest budget assessment. EA-6 with fresh budget will do D2 closure cleanly + D1 + 2.7 + 2.7.v2 + 2.8 + 2.9 + 2.10.
Stop discipline observed by EA-5: invariants preserved (HEAD 9277d61 unchanged; 21 M + 2 D + 9 ?? = 32 entries unchanged; state.json fleet_paused=true, last_updated_by="copilot_agent", last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" preserved; stash@{0} byte-identical sdo 20260424_013002). Single-commit discipline holds. No resume_fleet/pause_fleet cycle. No governance edits.

independent_disk_verification.check: git log --oneline -1: 9277d61 (zero new commits since EA-5 began). git status --porcelain: 32 entries (21 M + 2 D + 9 ??) — same set as v4 + LA's 9277d61 handoff commit; the 4 files EA-5 edited this session (server.py, legacy _vikunja_client.py, dashboard_maintainer.py, plus implicit re-saves) all already appear as M from prior 2.4 work and remain M. git stash list: stash@{0}: On main: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260424_013002 — byte-identical. state.json: fleet_paused=true; reason "platform separation v2 in progress"; last_updated_by="copilot_agent"; last_updated_utc="2026-04-24T20:46:12Z" — preserved. Single-commit discipline + fleet pause + stash invariance + governance untouched all hold.

Read tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py end-to-end: - Line 22: `from tools.fleet_observability._vikunja_client import LiveVikunjaClient` ✓ - Lines 108-127: existing_tasks dict + iteration loop with `live.update_task(...)` call - Lines 113-115: comment "Stage 2.6 D3 closure: was `live._request("POST", ...)` reaching into the private API; replaced with the named public ``update_task`` method on LiveVikunjaClient." ✓ - Lines 116-120: `live.update_task(int(existing_tasks[name]['id']), description=body, priority=5)` — clean named-method invocation; partial-update semantics via **fields kwargs ✓ - Line 122-127: `live.create_task(...)` — already a named-method call; unchanged.
Read tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py LiveVikunjaClient.update_task definition: - Lines 105-115: `def update_task(self, task_id: int, **fields: Any) -> None:` with docstring citing Stage 2.6 D3 closure rationale + recommendation to prefer typed wrappers (e.g., `update_task_priority`) where available ✓ - Implementation: `self._request("POST", f"/tasks/{task_id}", json=dict(fields))` ✓
Grep `(live|client)\._request` across tools/: 1 hit only — the comment at dashboard_maintainer.py:113 documenting the OLD code. Zero remaining (live|client)._request hits in active production code.
D3 is FULLY CLOSED. Decision-level acceptable: `update_task` named-method preserves explicit-API-surface discipline and the **fields kwarg signature is consistent with the partial-update use case.

Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 13-57 (the o1 decision documentation block): Decision: KEEP all 8 _require_project_id call sites at handler tops. DIVERGE from Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) recommendation. Rationale (4 points): 1. Handler call sites ARE the function-level chokepoint integration (not redundancy). _require_project_id is exported from chokepoint module tools/_vikunja_client.py and invoked at handler entry. Removing the calls would unhook this server from the chokepoint, not consolidate. 2. VikunjaClient CLASS is currently a stub: list_tasks/search_tasks methods call the gate but have NO HTTP delegation (per docstring at tools/_vikunja_client.py lines 50-53). Server.py uses _api_* helpers + httpx directly, never traverses VikunjaClient class. "Remove handler guards and rely on chokepoint-only" would functionally DELETE §D.1 enforcement at the MCP trust boundary. 3. True chokepoint-only (Option B) routing through VikunjaClient class methods would require implementing 13+ class methods + refactoring server.py to dispatch through instance — multi-deliverable refactor outside Item 2.6 D4 scope. 4. Test coverage doesn't change either way. Handler-call retention justified on trust-boundary architecture, not test-breakage avoidance.
INDEPENDENT VERIFICATION of EA-5's points: Point 1: VERIFIED. server.py line 117: `from tools._vikunja_client import _require_project_id`. The function is the import target; class is not imported. Function-level use is the chokepoint integration. Point 2: VERIFIED. tools/_vikunja_client.py: - Lines 50-53: docstring says "HTTP delegation is intentionally left as a stub at this stage; Stage 2.6 audits and migrates direct requests/httpx call sites to use this wrapper. The hard-rule enforcement is the Stage 2.1 deliverable." - Lines 59-68: list_tasks/search_tasks call _require_project_id but have "Delegation to the underlying HTTP client is wired in Stage 2.6" comments instead of HTTP code. server.py confirms: lines 113 (httpx import) + uses _api_* helpers throughout. Class never traversed. Point 3: VERIFIED via current state of tools/_vikunja_client.py (only list_tasks, search_tasks, get_task partially implemented; create_task, update_task, complete_task, etc. are listed in line 76 comment as "added incrementally as call sites are migrated" — i.e., not yet present). Point 4: VERIFIED. tools/tests/test_vikunja_client_scope.py tests _require_project_id (function) + class-method gates (e.g., test_list_with_string_project_id_raises invokes c.list_tasks(project_id="1")). No test exists that would break either way under D4.
DISPOSITION OF GUARDS PER EA-5: all 8 RETAINED, none added, none removed. No behavioral change. This block is EA-5's artifact-(a) lightweight evidence per Guide-#6's spec.
VERDICT: EA-5's DIVERGE is CORRECT. Guide-#6's recommendation was based on an incomplete reading of the current chokepoint state — the recommendation framed the handler guards as "redundant" (defense-in-depth on top of an active class chokepoint), but in current reality the class chokepoint is a stub and the handler-level function calls are the ACTIVE chokepoint. Removing them would weaken §D.1 enforcement, not consolidate it.
This is "treat as hypothesis; surface evidence; align or diverge with documented rationale" working correctly. EA-5 caught the Guide-side reasoning flaw via independent reading of the chokepoint state. Mature engineering. The lightweight artifact (code comment + closing handoff narrative per Guide-#6 answer to o1 evidence-form question) is the appropriate level of documentation for this divergence.

Read tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 12-104: Lines 12: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Lines 13-14: `Stage 2.6 deliverable 4 -- observation o1 routing decision (DIVERGE)` Lines 14-15: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Lines 16-21: References block (Guide review file path + Init pointer file path) Lines 22-56: Decision + 4-point rationale (the substance) Line 57: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Line 58: BLANK Line 59: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Lines 60-61: `Stage 2.6 deliverable 4 -- observation o1 routing decision (DIVERGE)` (DUPLICATE) Lines 62-63: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Lines 64-69: References block (DUPLICATE) Lines 70-102: Decision + 4-point rationale (DUPLICATE) Line 103: `------------------------------------------------------------------------------` Line 104: end of docstring
The entire 45-line decision block from line 13 to line 57 is reproduced VERBATIM at lines 59 to 103. This is an authoring artifact — most likely an Edit-tool replace operation that ran with a fragment that included both the new content AND a portion that overlapped, or a copy-paste sequence run twice.
Cleanup required: delete lines 58-103 inclusive. The remaining structure should be: Lines 12-57: single decision block (intro + rationale + disposition) Line 58 (was line 104): closing module docstring marker
Pattern reference: this is the same defect class as g5-7 (test_vikunja_client_scope.py duplicate test definitions caught by Guide-#5 and cleaned in EA-4 corrections cycle). EA-5 should perform the cleanup as a 30-second Edit operation before emitting v5.

Read tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py LiveVikunjaClient.__init__:
Lines 27-42: def __init__(self, base_url: str | None = None, timeout: float = 10.0) -> None: # Stage 2.6 deliverable 2 -- g5-4 thin-shim closure of the eager # ``config_loader.load_config()`` call that previously executed # unconditionally at every construction. The full fold of # ``LiveVikunjaClient`` into the chokepoint module # (``tools/_vikunja_client.py``) is deferred to Stage 5 per # Guide-#5 ack g5-4. Until then, the load is demoted to a fallback # path: callers that pass ``base_url`` explicitly never invoke # ``config_loader``, while the 6 zero-arg sites # (welcome_back_digest, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer, # daily_digest, credential_rotation_reminder, gate_stale_cleaner.run_live) # retain back-compat by relying on the fallback. if base_url is None: cfg = config_loader.load_config() base_url = cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"] self._base_url = base_url ...
The implementation: load_config call at line 40 is now CONDITIONAL on base_url=None, not always-on as before.
Test the disposition against ack g5-4 verbatim: ack g5-4: "if the script is invoked from devplatform cwd, the auto-resolve resolves to a non-BlarAI ctx and the overlay merge produces wrong allowlist. EA-4 MUST ensure the Item 2.6 disposition (whether 'fold entirely' or 'thin shim through new chokepoint') explicitly closes the line-28 load_config callsite before Item 2.9 single-commit. If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide."
Apply: - "load_config call in place": YES — the call is still present (line 40), just guarded by `if base_url is None`. - "without ctx threading": YES — EA-5 added a `base_url: str | None = None` parameter, NOT a `ctx: ProjectContext | None = None` parameter. The 6 callers still construct `LiveVikunjaClient()` zero-arg, which triggers the conditional fallback and invokes load_config zero-arg, which walks cwd, which from devplatform post-cutover resolves to devplatform's overlay (or fails). The auto-resolve hazard is unchanged for the production callers. - "w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide": YES, both clauses apply.
Independent disk verification: looked at dashboard_maintainer.py:93 (`live = LiveVikunjaClient()`, zero-arg). EA-5's session edit list does NOT include updating the 6 callers. Grep shows 6 zero-arg `LiveVikunjaClient()` invocations across the production path: - tools/fleet_observability/credential_rotation_reminder.py - tools/fleet_observability/welcome_back_digest.py - tools/fleet_observability/daily_digest.py - tools/fleet_observability/weekly_summary.py - tools/fleet_observability/dashboard_maintainer.py:93 - tools/gate_stale_cleaner/run_live.py None has been updated to pass base_url.
EA-5's framing of the disposition as "thin-shim closure of the EAGER call" misreads the hazard. The hazard isn't eagerness, it's ctx-unawareness. The conditional fallback preserves the ctx-unaware code path verbatim for the 6 zero-arg production callers.
ESCALATION TRIGGERED: per the explicit ack g5-4 rule, this IS a w15 hazard requiring Guide escalation. THIS REVIEW IS THAT ESCALATION.
REQUIRED CLOSURE PATH (defer to EA-6 per v5 handoff approval below): Update each of the 6 zero-arg callers to pass base_url explicitly. Each caller already has `cfg` resolved via ctx-aware load_config call (e.g., dashboard_maintainer.py:85-92). The change is a 1-line update per caller: BEFORE: live = LiveVikunjaClient() AFTER: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) Total: ~6 lines. Run pytest tools/ to verify no regression (expect 282 passed; no V matrix tests target this code path so count should hold).
Optional (not required at this stage): pass ctx itself into LiveVikunjaClient and have its constructor call load_config(path=ctx.root/..., project_overlay_path=ctx.root/...). This is a richer ctx-threading approach but would expand the constructor signature and require updating callers anyway. The base_url-only update is the minimal viable closure; full ctx-threading is appropriate at Stage 5 cleanup full-fold.
DISPOSITION RE-DECLARATION (clarifies EA-5's framing for the audit chain): EA-5's "thin-shim conditional load_config" closes the EAGER pattern but does NOT close the g5-4 hazard. Per ack g5-4, the disposition is INCOMPLETE. The 6-caller update is the closure path. EA-6 executes this as the first action before D1 grep verification.

EA-5 stopped at the inter-deliverable breakpoint and asked the LA for direction rather than autonomously emitting v5. This matches the stop_discipline pattern + i1-4 + Guide-#5 "even slightly tight" trigger. EA-5 framed the v5-vs-continue decision as a clear either/or with a stated default, which is exactly the LA-readable summary the LA + Guide can act on quickly. Mature handoff discipline.

correction.topic: D4 documentation duplicate comment block in server.py
correction.issue: tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py contains a DUPLICATE 45-line decision block at lines 13-57 AND lines 59-103 (verbatim duplicate, including the closing rule + opening rule). Authoring artifact analogous to g5-7 dup test definitions in test_vikunja_client_scope.py caught by Guide-#5.
correction.required_action: EA-5 deletes lines 58-103 inclusive (the blank line + the entire second copy block). Result: single decision block from line 13 to line 57; the original module imports begin at the next non-blank line. Re-run pytest tools/ to verify import + parse OK; expect 282 passed (no test count change since the docstring change has no runtime effect). This is a ~30-second Edit operation; too cheap to defer to EA-6.

correction.topic: D2 g5-4 hazard not closed for 6 zero-arg callers
correction.issue: EA-5 made load_config CONDITIONAL in legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py.__init__ (line 39-41) but did NOT update the 6 zero-arg production callers (credential_rotation_reminder, welcome_back_digest, daily_digest, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer, gate_stale_cleaner.run_live). The 6 callers still construct LiveVikunjaClient() zero-arg, which still triggers the conditional fallback, which still invokes load_config zero-arg, which still walks cwd. Post-Stage-4 cutover from devplatform cwd, this resolves to devplatform overlay (not BlarAI overlay) — the exact hazard ack g5-4 explicitly flagged.
correction.source_citation: EA-5 disposition file docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/2.4-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_5_VERIFICATION_OF_EA_2.4_PARTIAL.xml ack g5-4: "If 2.6 ends up leaving the load_config call in place without ctx threading, that is a w15 hazard and requires escalation back to Guide." Verified on disk: tools/fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py line 40 still contains `cfg = config_loader.load_config()` zero-arg; the 6 callers still invoke `LiveVikunjaClient()` zero-arg.
correction.required_action:
Defer to EA-6 per v5 handoff approval. EA-6's first action is to update the 6 callers as follows: BEFORE: live = LiveVikunjaClient() AFTER: live = LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"]) Each caller already has `cfg` resolved via ctx-aware load_config call. Total ~6 lines. Run pytest tools/ to verify; expect 282 passed.
EA-5's v5 mid-stage handoff XML MUST explicitly encode this incompleteness in the successor_immediate_next_actions block, with: - clear statement that D2 disposition is INCOMPLETE per ack g5-4 - the 6-caller closure list verbatim - the BEFORE/AFTER pattern verbatim - the pytest sanity check AND in the work_remaining block, list this as deliverable "D2-completion" preceding D1.
correction.do_at: EA-5 v5 XML: encode incompleteness + closure path. EA-6: execute closure as first substantive action (after V0 pickup verification).

commendations_for_ea_5.commendation: EA-5 did not rubber-stamp Guide-#6's chokepoint-only (B) recommendation. EA-5 read the actual chokepoint state (VikunjaClient class is a stub; HTTP delegation deferred), read server.py end-to-end (uses _api_* helpers + httpx directly, never traverses VikunjaClient), and concluded that Guide-#6's recommendation was based on a misread of the current reality. EA-5's 4-point rationale is technically correct and correctly identifies that handler-level _require_project_id calls ARE the chokepoint integration, not redundancy on top of an active class chokepoint. This is exactly the LA's "treat as hypothesis; surface evidence; align or diverge with documented rationale" mandate working as intended. Mature engineering with appropriate audit-chain documentation.

Clean named-method API on LiveVikunjaClient with appropriate docstring noting the preference for typed wrappers. The dashboard_maintainer.py callsite update (3-line change to multi-line invocation with task_id, description, priority) is well-formatted and includes a comment block explaining the Stage 2.6 D3 closure rationale + the OLD _request pattern. Zero remaining (live|client)._request hits in production code per grep verification.

EA-5 stopped before autonomous v5 emission and asked LA + Guide for direction. The framing of the v5-vs-continue decision as either/or with a stated default + clear rationale is the right discipline. Honest token budget assessment + recognition that mid-2.7 test-run interruption would be worse than v5-now both reflect mature judgment.

No new commits, no governance edits, no resume_fleet/pause_fleet cycle, stash byte-identical, working tree count exactly the same 32 entries. All Stage 2 invariants preserved across 3 substantive edits.

v5_handoff_authorization.rationale:
EA-5's i1-4-grounded recommendation (clean inter-deliverable breakpoint at the structural- decisions-done boundary; D1 grep is mechanical and naturally fits the next session's first action; mid-2.7 test-run interruption is worse than v5-now) is sound. Per i1-4 + dn15: "even slightly tight" budget at clean breakpoint = handoff. EA-5 has been operating for multiple substantive edits + Guide-#6 carry-forward processing + 33-ack restatement + D1-D5 v4 corrections internalization. The honest budget assessment is credible.
However, two cleanups must precede v5 emission: (a) Duplicate D4 comment block in server.py — trivial (~30 sec). Refusing to do this on budget grounds is unjustifiable; it removes a documentation defect from EA-6's inheritance set. (b) D2 incompleteness encoding in v5 XML — moderate (~5-10 min of v5 XML drafting). Without explicit encoding, EA-6 might inherit "EA-5 said D2 is substantively complete" and miss the 6-caller closure. The v5 XML must list D2-completion as deliverable preceding D1 in work_remaining + describe the BEFORE/AFTER pattern in successor_immediate_next_actions.
The D2 closure WORK ITSELF (6-caller updates; ~30-45 min) is appropriate to DEFER to EA-6. EA-6 with fresh budget will do D2 closure cleanly as a first action, then D1 grep verification (mechanical, ~30-60 min), then 2.7 (pytest), 2.7.v2 (V matrix V4-V7 NEW tests authoring), 2.8 ($BLARAI_PID==3 hard gate), 2.9 (single Stage-2 commit folding ALL work; pyproject.toml MUST be in commit), 2.10 (append-only STATUS).

emission_protocol.step: EA-5 deletes lines 58-103 of tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py (the duplicate comment block) via Edit tool. Verify result is a single 44-line decision block ending at line ~57.

EA-5 runs pytest tools/ --tb=short -q. Expect 282 passed (the dup cleanup is docstring- only; no behavioral change; no test count change). Report the result in the v5 XML ground_truth_at_handoff block.

EA-5 drafts docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v5.xml following v1/v2/v3/v4 schema verbatim (use v4 as the closest template). Required sections: - metadata (predecessor=4, successor=5 or 6 [next EA instance], emitted_at_utc, head_at_handoff_before_this_commit=9277d61, head_at_handoff_after_this_commit (the v5 commit), trigger_reason, items_completed=2.1..2.5.v2 + D2-partial + D3 + D4) - ground_truth_at_handoff (post-cleanup git status, pytest 282 confirmed, stash byte-identical, state.json invariants preserved) - work_completed_inherited (carry forward v1/v2/v3/v4 inherited block) - work_completed_this_session (D2-partial + D3 + D4 with explicit reference to this Guide-#6 review file at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ 2.6-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_6_INTER_DELIVERABLE_REVIEW_EA_5_D2_INCOMPLETE_V5_AUTHORIZED.xml) - work_remaining: list "D2-completion" FIRST as a NEW first deliverable (preceding D1), with the 6-caller closure list + BEFORE/AFTER pattern verbatim from this Guide-#6 review's correction-2 required_action block. Then D1 (extended grep) + 2.7 + 2.7.v2 + 2.8 + 2.9 + 2.10. - standing_rules_carried_forward - critical_carryovers (33 acks restated; SATISFIED tags updated with the new D3, D4-decision, D4-doc-cleanup status; FORWARD-ACTIVE for g5-4 D2-completion) - successor_pickup_verification (V0a-V0k + new V0l = `git status --porcelain | wc -l` should still be 32 entries since cleanup is in-place + no new files) - successor_immediate_next_actions (V0 verification → comprehension gate → D2-closure 6-caller update → D1 grep → 2.7-2.10) - handoff_metadata_for_audit (decision_log adds: D4-divergence rationale citation + D2-incomplete framing per Guide-#6 review)

Single docs-only commit with ONLY the v5 XML in diff. Commit message: docs(platform_separation): Stage 2 mid-stage EA handoff v5 -- D3 + D4 decision (DIVERGE) closed; D2 partial (load_config conditional but 6 callers still zero-arg, w15 escalation per g5-4); D1+2.7-2.10 deferred to successor Verify post-commit: git log --oneline -1 shows v5 commit; git show --stat shows ONE file in diff (the v5 XML); git status --porcelain shows the same 32 entries unchanged + dup-cleanup landed in server.py M.
EA-5 reports v5 commit hash to LA + cites this Guide-#6 review file path. EA-5 then retires.

required_acknowledgments.ack: EA-5 acknowledges the duplicate decision block in tools/vikunja_mcp/server.py lines 58-103 will be deleted via Edit operation before v5 XML drafting. After the deletion, EA-5 will re-read the cleaned file to confirm the single 44-line block remains well-formed and the module imports at line 105+ are unchanged.

ack.topic: D2 incompleteness encoding in v5 XML

required_acknowledgments.ack: EA-5 acknowledges that D2 disposition is INCOMPLETE per ack g5-4 because the line-40 load_config call is still triggered by 6 zero-arg callers, and that the v5 mid-stage handoff XML MUST explicitly encode: - D2-completion as a NEW first deliverable in work_remaining (preceding D1) - the 6-caller closure list verbatim (credential_rotation_reminder, welcome_back_digest, daily_digest, weekly_summary, dashboard_maintainer:93, gate_stale_cleaner.run_live) - the BEFORE/AFTER pattern verbatim (LiveVikunjaClient() → LiveVikunjaClient(base_url=cfg["vikunja"]["base_url"])) - the pytest sanity check (expect 282 passed; no count change) - reference to this Guide-#6 review file as the source of the D2-incompleteness finding

EA-5 acknowledges that the v5 commit MUST be docs-only (one file in diff: the v5 XML under ea_outputs/) per i1-4 + dn15. The dup-cleanup edit to server.py + any other working-tree state remains UNCOMMITTED for EA-6 to fold into the Stage-2 single commit at item 2.9.

EA-5 acknowledges Guide-#6 has APPROVED the D4 divergence (chokepoint-function-as-integration, not chokepoint-class-routing). The decision retention applies forward; future EA sessions or Stage 5 cleanup may revisit if VikunjaClient class HTTP delegation is wired and the D4 reasoning conditions change.

standing_reminders_for_ea_5_pre_v5_emiss.reminder: Single Stage-2 commit at item 2.9 only; v5 mid-stage handoff is the EXEMPT docs-only commit (one file in diff: the v5 XML).
stash@{0} (sdo 20260424_013002) is UNTOUCHED — never git stash pop / git stash apply / git stash drop. v5 ground_truth_at_handoff must record the byte-identical timestamp.

The v5 XML schema MUST follow v1/v2/v3/v4 pattern. Schema deviation hints at lower-quality handoff. Use v4 as the closest template; v4 is at docs/platform_separation/ea_outputs/EA_HANDOFF_STAGE2_MIDSTAGE_v4.xml.
The v5 successor_pickup_verification block must predict exactly 32 entries (21 M + 2 D + 9 ??) post-cleanup -- the dup-cleanup edit to server.py is in-place (still M, no count change).
The v5 work_remaining block must list D2-completion as deliverable BEFORE D1. EA-6 picks up D2 closure as first substantive action (after V0 pickup verification + iteration-1 comprehension gate).

guide_review_response.proceed_authorization:
PROCEED to v5 emission via the 5-step protocol in <v5_handoff_authorization> <emission_protocol>. Step 1 (dup cleanup) and Step 2 (pytest sanity) are mandatory pre-flight; Steps 3-5 are the v5 drafting + commit + report cycle. After v5 commit lands, EA-5 retires; LA spawns EA-6 with the standard attachment set + v5 handoff + this Guide-#6 review file + the Guide-#6 EA-5 init pointer + the v4 quality review + the 9 Guide-#5 disposition files + STATUS.md + cross-references.
If the v5 drafting itself surfaces any unexpected state (e.g., pytest fails after dup cleanup, or the dup cleanup has unintended side effects), STOP and report. The dup cleanup is a docstring-only change with no runtime effect; pytest 282 should hold.
If EA-5's budget tightens further during v5 drafting, the v5 XML can be smaller than v4 — the schema requires the listed sections but the prose density can be reduced. Reference Guide-#6 review file + ack g5-4 by path; do not duplicate verbatim text.
